name: 2004 Iranian Parliamentary Election (7th Majlis)
description: The February 2004 parliamentary election was marked by the [Guardian Council](/orgs/guardian-council)'s mass disqualification of approximately 2,400 candidates, including most reformist incumbents and all candidates of the [Islamic Iran Participation Front](/orgs/islamic-iran-participation-front). Approximately 80 sitting MPs were barred from seeking re-election. The result was a sweeping conservative victory, with the [Abadgaran](/orgs/alliance-of-builders-of-islamic-iran) coalition winning over 190 seats. Speaker [Gholam-Ali Haddad-Adel](/people/gholam-ali-haddad-adel) was elected with 226 of 259 votes, becoming the first non-clerical Speaker since the revolution. Turnout collapsed to around 51%.
editorial_notes: Key event demonstrating Guardian Council as electoral engineering body. Triggered mass reformist calls to boycott or protest the election. Khatami condemned the election as unfair but allowed it to proceed.
